title: scanner load_state: witnesses loaded against empty tree after tree deserialization failure
description: In wallet/src/scanner.rs load_state() (lines 322-352), when read_commitment_tree() fails to deserialize the stored tree, the code logs a warning and leaves self.tree as an empty CommitmentTree, then continues to load per-note witnesses from the DB (via spendable_notes). These witnesses are IncrementalWitness instances forked from the previous (now non-empty) tree state. With an empty self.tree and witnesses rooted in an incompatible (non-empty) tree, the next scan_block() will append new commitments to the empty tree and call witness.append() on each existing witness — but those witnesses' internal position is relative to the old tree depth. The result is silently corrupted witnesses that produce invalid Merkle proofs, making notes unspendable with no clear error at spend time. Fix: if tree deserialization fails, do not load witnesses (they are also invalid); clear self.witnesses, or propagate the tree error as an Err so spawn_scanner refuses to proceed.
